original: During a snowstorm at Fort Keogh, Montana, the world's largest snowflakes are reported to be 15 inches (38 cm) wide and 8 inches (20 cm) thick
The Lego Group patents their design of interlocking Lego bricks, still compatible with bricks produced today
Johanne Relleke gets stung by bees 2,443 times in Rhodesia & survives
Bernie Sandersmittens worn to the inauguration raise $1.8 million for Vermont charities after images go viral
Record 295,000 dominoes toppled in Fuerth, West Germany
Iran’s Pishgam rocket successfully completes a return trip of sending a monkey into space
Johan van Hulst, Dutch teacher who helped save 600 children from the Nazis, born in Amsterdam, Netherlands (d. 2018)

American and Bermudan naturalist discover nesting site of Bermuda petrel, a sea bird also known as cahow; the species was thought extinct since 1615, but sporadic sightings spawned an expedition to some remote rocky crags off of Bermuda
1st photograph bounced off Moon, Washington, D.C.
Charity single "We Are the World" recorded by supergroup USA for Africa (Michael Jackson, Lionel Richie,Quincy Jones,Bruce Springsteen,Stevie Wonder, and other pop stars)
The first locomotive runs from the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean on the Panama Railway
Welsh pirateHenry Morgancaptures Panama City from its Spanish defenders
London's Pall Mall becomes the first street lit by gaslight
WW II Navy flier Don Mason sends message "Sighted sub sank same"
Jane Austen's "Pride and Prejudice" is published by Thomas Egerton in the United Kingdom
Bal des Ardents - fire during a royal court ball in Paris kills four dancers dressed as wild men, with KingCharles VInarrowly escaping
Seven survivors from missing Kiribati ferry carrying 100 people rescued after week at sea
Australian Open Women's Tennis:Serena Williamsdefeats older sisterVenus Williams6–4, 6–4 for her 7th Australian title and record 23rd Grand Slam event singles victory
Edmonton centerWayne Gretzky's NHL record point scoring streak ends at 51 games when Oilers beaten 4-2 by LA Kings; Gretzky totals 61-92-153 during the period
DNA analysis confirms that the 6th century Plague of Justinian was caused by a variant of Yersinia pestis (the same bacteria for the Black Death)
Auguste Piccard, Swiss scientist, explorer and balloonist (1st flight into stratosphere, by balloon), born in Basel, Switzerland (d. 1962)
Jean Felix Piccard, Swiss scientist, explorer and balloonist (1st flight into stratosphere, by balloon), born in Basel, Switzerland (d. 1963)
Eugène Dubois, Dutch paleontologist who discovered human ancestor Pithecanthropus erectus "Java Man", born in Eisden, Netherlands (d. 1940)[1]
In his State of the Union address US PresidentGeorge W. Bushannounces President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) - plan will go on to save 25 million lives worldwide (as of Jan 2023)
Manitoba women are the first in Canada to win the right to vote and to hold provincial office
British colonial officerStamford Raffleslands in Singapore
Thai boxer Chartchai Chionoi retains his world flyweight title with a 13th-round TKO of home favourite Efren Torres in Mexico City; first of 3 meetings between the pair
Soviet Union wins 62-37 for 1st international basketball loss by US
NY Giants sign their 1st black players, Monte Irvin & Ford Smith
St. Louis right wingBrett Hullbecomes 2nd player (after Wayne Gretzky) in NHL history to record back-to-back seasons of 50 goals in 50 games; #50 comes in 3-3 Blues' tie in Los Angeles
England cricket spin bowler Tom Hartley follows Ollie Pope's 196 with stunning debut figures of 7/62 as the tourists upset India by 28 runs in the 1st Test in Hyderabad
The West Indies win their first cricket Test match in Australia in 27 years in Brisbane; rookie fast bowler Shamar Joseph takes 7/68 to guide Windies to an 8 run victory and a 1-1 series tie
Inna Lassovskaja jumps ladies world record (14.78m)
Chicago's Les Cunningham scores 5 points in one period to set an NHL record that stands until 1978; records 2 goals & 3 assists in a 10:04 span during the 3d period of an 8-1 win over Montreal
Super Bowl XXIV, Louisiana Superdome, New Orleans, LA: San Francisco 49ers beat Denver Broncos, 55-10; MVP:Joe Montana, SF, QB
1971 NFL Draft: Jim Plunkett from Stanford University first pick by New England Patriots
NFL announces Dallas Cowboys (1960) & Minnesota Vikings (1961) franchises
WWII: 683 British RAF bombers attack Berlin, Germany
Swedish ships bring food to starving Netherlands
"The Who" make their 1st appearance on British television programme "Ready Steady Go!"
Elvis Presley's 1st appearance on national TV on the Dorsey Brothers's "Stage Show"
Sarah McLachlan, Canadian Juno and Grammy Award-winning folk singer-songwriter (Fumbling Towards Ecstasy; "I Will Remember You"), born in Halifax, Nova Scotia
Harry Corbett, English puppeteer (Sooty), TV presenter and magician, born in Bradford, England (d. 1989)
Luther Simjian, Armenian-American Inventor (teleprompter and first self-posing portrait camera), born in Aintab, Ottoman Empire (d. 1997)
Hidetsugu Yagi, Japanese electrical engineer (Yagi-Uda antenna), born in Osaka, Empire of Japan (d. 1976)
William Seward Burroughs, American inventor of the first workable adding machine and the electric alarm clock, born in Rochester, New York (d. 1898)
British writer Horace Walpole, in a letter to Horace Mann, coins the word serendipity
Yale Daily News is published, the first college daily newspaper
The United States Coast Guard is created by merging the US Life Saving Service & the US Revenue Cutter Service
Commission forAuguste Rodin's sculpture 'The Burghers of Calais' signed by the Municipal Council of Calais.
The Russian Academy of Sciences founded in St. Petersburg byPeter the Great, and implemented in a Senate decree. Called St. Petersburg Academy of Sciences until 1917.
9-year-oldEdward VIsucceedsHenry VIIIas King of England
Sir Thomas Warner founds the first English colony in the Caribbean, on Saint Kitts.
Republic of Rwanda proclaimed
100 Ballads names "The Wandering Prince of Troy" the most popular ballad of the 17th century in a project documenting "the earliest form of commercial pop music in England"
-34°F (-37°C), Cynthiana, Kentucky (state record)
-46°F (-43°C), Pittsburgh, New Hampshire (state record)
29th PGA Seniors Golf Championship: Chandler Harper
Mickey Welch, George Kelly & Billy Evans elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Construction begins on 1st private thorium-uranium nuclear reactor
